title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Ridesdale provides Miss Marple with a transcript of Coradine's conversation with witnesses in Chipping Cleghorn to obtain her opinion on the case. After reading the report, Miss Marple observes that the witnesses' accounts are complicated by trivial details. She notes that because there was no light in the hallway or on the stairs, the witnesses would have seen only the beam of a flashlight held by a masked man, rather than the man himself. This leads her to suggest that Rudi Scherz is a "scapegoat" who was hired to perform a prank. She theorizes that Scherz was paid to place a newspaper advertisement to investigate the mansion, arrive wearing a mask and cloak, and wave a flashlight while shouting "Hands up!" 

Miss Marple proposes that Scherz did not actually possess a revolver. Instead, she suggests that after he shouted, a mysterious person approached him in the dark, placed a gun over his shoulder, and fired two shots. This person then threw the gun next to Scherz to frame him. Miss Marple asserts that the motive was to kill Miss Blacklock and suggests that Mona Harris might have information regarding who orchestrated the plan.

Colonel Draco goes to interview Mona Harris. Mona Harris explains to Inspector Corrado that she and Scherz had planned to go to the movies, but Scherz canceled to pull a "prank" involving a staged robbery. She had seen the advertisement he placed in the newspaper and initially thought it was a joke. She denies knowing he possessed a revolver and expresses fear that her prior knowledge of his plans will make her appear to be a premeditated accomplice.
